title: Bitter Gooey Algerian Milk Dumplings
description: A show‑off, FODMAP‑friendly dessert that blends bitter cocoa with gooey milk‑based dumplings, finished with a saffron‑infused maple glaze and toasted pistachios.
instructions: Whisk the lactose‑free milk with cocoa powder until smooth.
In a separate bowl, combine semolina, oat flour, baking powder, and salt; mix well.
Add melted butter and vanilla extract to the milk mixture and stir to combine.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and knead until a soft dough forms.
Let the dough rest for 10 minutes.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil; drop spoonfuls of the dough into the pot and simmer for 8–10 minutes until the dumplings rise and become translucent.
Heat the maple syrup with a pinch of saffron in a small saucepan until slightly thickened.
Drain the dumplings and transfer to a serving dish; drizzle with the saffron maple glaze.
Sprinkle chopped pistachios on top and serve warm.
